title: Fluorescently labeled polyamine uptake (via flow cytometry)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b42hb","text":"Assess polyamine uptake capacities of a specific cell line after incubation with fluorescently labeled polyamines and mean fluorescence intensitiy acquisition via flow cytometry. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":159,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e7dec","text":"Ensure fluorescent materials are protected from light during manipulation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egkkt","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"aekr9","text":"Prepare appropriate dilution of fluorescently labeled polyamine in cell culture medium.\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"h2nh","text":"Prepare Flow Cytometry (FC) buffer made of 1% Albumin Fraction V in DPBS (-/-).\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6q7mt","text":"Prepare Eppendorf tubes and FC tubes by labeling them and keeping them on ice. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7t31g","text":"BODIPY-putrescine (Cat# SCT248, Merck)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"do0ia","text":"BODIPY-spermidine (Cat# SCT249, Merck)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cp02f","text":"BODIPY-spermine (Cat# SCT250, Merck)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d5og3","text":"Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) Fraction V (Cat# 8076.4, Carl Roth)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fb9fl","text":"Dulbecco's Phosphate Buffered Saline without calcium chloride and magnesium chloride (DPBS) (Cat# 14190144, GIBCO)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bs8av","text":"TrypLE (Cat# 12604021, GIBCO)\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bs5f7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The day of the experiment, remove cell culture medium and add fluorescently labeled polyamines to the cells in a final volume of 1000 µL, / well . Concentration and incubation time may vary depending on the cell type. It is recommended to test various incubation times and doses to better understand the kinetics. Keep protected from light.
Incubate at 37 °C with 5% CO₂ for the desired duration, ensuring protection from light.
Add 500 µL, /well 1%-Albumin Fraction V in DPBS, collect the cells and transfer in Eppendorf tubes on ice.
Discard supernatants and resuspend cell pellets in 500 µL 1%-Albumin Fraction V in DPBS, depending on the size of the cell pellet.
Keep on ice until acquisition at the flow cytometer. Record 10,000 live events per sample.
Add 200 µL, /well TrypLE. Incubate 00:05:00, at RT protected from light.
Seed cells in a 12-well plate so they reach 70-80% confluency on the day of the experiment. This protocol is made for a 12 well-plate format. Adaptations need to be done to scale up or down.
Harvest cells and prepare samples for Flow Cytometry (FC).
Filter cell suspension through Nylon filter into FC tubes.
creator: Sarah van Veen
